name: Mackmyra Svensk Whisky
notes: First Swedish whisky distillery; uses Swedish barley, juniper smoke, and Swedish oak casks. Gravity-fed distillery designed for sustainability.

name: High Coast (formerly Box)
notes: Sub-arctic distillery at 63°N; extreme temperature swings accelerate maturation. Uses Swedish barley from Norrland fields.

name: Smögen Whisky
notes: West coast craft distillery; heavily peated Swedish barley. Floor-malted heritage approach. Named after fishing village.

name: Spirit of Hven
notes: Island distillery on Hven (between Sweden and Denmark); grows own barley on-site. Organic estate distillery with heritage barley varieties.

name: Stauning Whisky
notes: Danish craft distillery; floor-malts own grain including heritage Danish rye. Direct-fire distillation. Co-founded by 9 friends.

name: Kyrö Distillery
notes: Finnish rye whiskey pioneer; uses 100% Finnish whole-grain rye. Born in a sauna conversation. World's northernmost rye whiskey.

name: Teerenpeli
notes: Finland's oldest whisky distillery; attached to restaurant/brewery. Finnish barley matured in Finnish conditions.

name: Myken Destilleri
notes: World's second-northernmost distillery on a tiny island (66°N) with 9 inhabitants. Arctic maritime maturation. Norwegian barley.

name: Aurora Spirit
notes: World's northernmost distillery (69°N); under the Northern Lights. Norwegian barley; Arctic aquavit tradition alongside whisky.

name: Eimverk Distillery
notes: Iceland's first whisky distillery; uses Icelandic barley dried with sheep dung smoke (ancient tradition). Flóki brand.

name: Bruichladdich
notes: Islay distillery leading terroir-driven Scotch; sources heritage barley varieties (Bere, Concerto, Odyssey). 'The Botanist' gin from foraged botanicals.

name: Waterford Distillery
notes: Terroir-obsessed Irish distillery; sources 100+ individual farm barley lots. Tracks variety and field of origin. Heritage varieties including Hunter and Taberna.

name: Westland Distillery
notes: American single malt pioneer; uses heritage barley varieties and Washington-grown grain. Collaborations with WSU barley breeding program.

name: Koval Distillery
notes: Organic craft distillery; grain-to-bottle with Midwest heritage rye and wheat. First distillery in Chicago since Prohibition.

name: Copperworks Distilling
notes: American single malt from Pacific Northwest malted barley. Brewer-turned-distiller approach using craft brewing heritage.
